titleStmt.title: Bǝḥerāwi Kǝllǝlāwi Mangǝśti Tǝgrāy, ʿUrā Qirqos, UM-050
Fragments of ten various manuscripts

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msContents.summary:
I-1) Homily by the Orthodox on St John the Baptist (fols. 43r-44v, possibly fragments of two different texts from the same Ms.), hand a (28 lines) II) Homilies (fols. 45r-122v), hand b (identical to hand e of UM-045) (25 lines) II-1) Possibly the Homily by Jacob for the third Sunday in Lent (fols. 45r-59ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 5.) II-2) Homily on Lent, for the third Sunday in Lent (fols. 59rb-66vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 6.) II-3) Homily by John Chrysostom for the fourth Sunday in Lent (fols. 66vb-69ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 7.) II-4) Homily by the Orthodox on Lent, for the fourth Sunday in Lent (69ra-73ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 8.) II-5) Homily by Gregory of Antioch for the fifth Sunday in Lent (fols. 73ra-79ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 9.) II-6) Homily for the Friday of the sixth week in Lent (fols. 79ra-84vb) (Orient. 8192, 10.; beginning in the same way, without title)
II-7) Homily for the Saturday of the sixth week in Lent (fols. 73ra-85vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 11.) II-8) Homily by Jacob (ʾabba Yāʿqob) for the Sunday of the sixth week in Lent (fols. 85vb-91ra) (Orient. 8192, 12.) II-9) Homily by the Orthodox for Palm Sunday (fols. 91ra-97ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 13.)
II-10) Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for reading at the service on Holy Thursday (fols. 97ra-103vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 14.) III) Martyrdom of St George of Lydda (fols. 98rv, 142r-148v), hand c (27-28 lines) (identical to hand d of UM-045) (cp. E.A.W. BUDGE, The History of George of Lydda, the patron Saint of England, London 1930, respectively pp. 89 l.17-91 l. 4 and pp. 96 l. 12-108 l. 18) II-11) Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for reading at the service on Holy Thursday (fols. 97ra-103vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 14) II-12) Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for Easter Eve (fols. 103vb-107vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 15., beginning in the same way), in full II-13) Homily by the Orthodox for Easter Sunday (fols. 107vb-118va) (cp. Orient. 8192, 16.), in full II-14) Homily on the Suffering of Jesus Christ for Monday in Easter-week (fols. 118vb-122va, fol. 122 is loose), incomplete (cp. Orient. 8192, 17.) I-2) Text containing God’s promises to Israel (fol. 123rv, a loose leaf), hand a (27 lines in a column) IV-1) Homily by Cyril (Gerlos) Patriarch of Jerusalem on the Resurrection of Jesus Christ (fol. 124r-131v, after the incipit page, the biggest part possibly missing), hand d (25 lines) IV-2) Discourse by John Chrysostom on the life and decollation of John the Baptist (fols. 126r-132v), possibly almost complete, hand d V-1) A homily for Easter (fol. 133rv), ancient hand e VI-1) 2 Kings (fol. 134rv), ancient hand f (similar but not identical with hand a of UM-045) IV-3) Maṣḥafa lǝdatā la-Māryām “Book of the Nativity of Mary” (fols. 135, 136va-141v), end missing, hand d (Cp. M. CHAINE, Apocrypha de Beata Maria Virgine, Romae 1909 [CSCO 39, 40 (SAe 22, 23)], “Liber nativitatis Mariae”; the correct sequence is fols. 136va-141v, 135) IV-4) Martyrdom of Theocritus the Reader, commemorated on 17 Maggābit (fol. 136r-va), end part and explicit, hand d VII-1) Text concerning ʾabbā Yoḥannǝs (?) (fol. 149rv): hand g (28 lines) VIII-1) The Finding of the Holy Cross by St Helena (?) (fols. 150r-152r, explicit), hand h (35 lines) V-2) Unidentified homily (fol. 152rv), ancient hand e (31 lines) IX-1) 2 Kings 18:32-19:15 (fol. 153rv), ancient hand j (35 lines) (identical to the hand of UM-058, the text follows on UM-058 fol. 66rv)
I-3) Text concerning the Nativity of John the Baptist (fol. 154rv), hand a (28 lines) I-4) Prayer concerning Yoḥannǝs (Maṭmǝq) (?) (fol. 155rv): hand a (?) (27 lines) V-3) Homily by John Chrysostom on Easter Sunday (fols. 156ra-161ra), in full (?), ancient hand e (32 lines) (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 17, HamSixBerl 1, 298-99) V-4) Homily by an anonymous author on Easter (fols. 161ra-161vb), 16th-cent. hand k; the text is written in empty space after Text V-3, and may be the beginning of the following text (which had been acephalous, originally or though the loss of leaves) V-5) Unidentified homily (fols. 162r-167v), acephalos (but cp. above), ancient hand e (31 lines) X) Text concerning Archangel Michael, the Vision of Daniel and the Three Holy Children (fol. 168r-169v), hand l (28 lines) V-6) Unidentified homily (fols. 171r-171v), end part and explicit, ancient hand e (31 lines) V-7) A homily of Jacob (of Sarug?) (fol. 177vb), heading only, ancient hand e V-8) Unidentified homily (fols. 172ra-173ra), explicit, ancient hand e V-8) Homily by John Chrysostom on penance (fols. 173ra-185v), ancient hand e (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 25, HamSixBerl 1, 300) I-2) Text concerning John the Baptist (fols. 186r-187v): hand a (28 lines) V-9) Unidentified homily (fols. 188ra-189rb), end part and explicit, ancient hand e (31 lines) V-10) Homily by Jacob of Serug on the Apostle Thomas (fols. 189r-194v), in full (?), ancient hand e (cp. EAE III, 262b) V-11) Homily by John Chrysostom on Lazarus (fols. 194va-197rb), in full (?), ancient hand e (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 20, HamSixBerl 1, 299; cp. EAE III, 293b) V-12) Homily by John Chrysostom on the Samaritan woman (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 21, HamSixBerl 1, 299; Orient. 774 fols. 126r-131r, EAE III, 293b), in full (?), ancient hand e V-13) Homily by John Theologus (Tewologos) on Easter Sunday (fols. 205vb-211vb), end and some fols. missing, ancient hand e (cp. Orient. 774, no. 22).

supportDesc.foliation: Foliated through together with Ms. UM-50a (Ms. UM-50a has ff. 1-42, UM-59 has ff. 43-203)
supportDesc.condition: The fragments of ten manuscripts catalogued as UM-50 are loosely placed into the binding of Ms. UM-50a (folia 1-42).

msContents.summary: I-1) Homily by the Orthodox on St. John the Baptist (fols. 43r-44v, possibly fragments of two different texts from the same Ms.), hand a (28 lines) I-2) Text containing God’s promises to Israel (fol. 123rv, a loose leaf), hand a (27 lines in a column) I-3) Text concerning the Nativity of John the Baptist (fol. 154rv), hand a (28 lines) I-4) Prayer concerning Yoḥannǝs (Maṭmǝq) (?) (fol. 155rv): hand a (?) (27 lines) I-2) Text concerning John the Baptist (fols. 186r-187v): hand a (28 lines)

msItem.title: Homily by the Orthodox on St. John the Baptist
msItem.note: possibly fragments of two different texts from the same Ms

msItem.title: Text containing God’s promises to Israel (fol. 123rv, a loose leaf)
Text concerning the Nativity of John the Baptist
Prayer concerning Yoḥannǝs (Maṭmǝq) (?)
Text concerning John the Baptist

msContents.summary: II) Homilies (fols. 45r-122v), hand b (identical to hand e of UM-045) (25 lines): II-1) Possibly the Homily by Jacob for the third Sunday in Lent (fols. 45r-59ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 5.) II-2) Homily on Lent, for the third Sunday in Lent (fols. 59rb-66vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 6.) II-3) Homily by John Chrysostom for the fourth Sunday in Lent (fols. 66vb-69ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 7.) II-4) Homily by the Orthodox on Lent, for the fourth Sunday in Lent (69ra-73ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 8.) II-5) Homily by Gregory of Antioch for the fifth Sunday in Lent (fols. 73ra-79ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 9.) II-6) Homily for the Friday of the sixth week in Lent (fols. 79ra-84vb) (Orient. 8192, 10.; beginning in the same way, without title) II-7) Homily for the Saturday of the sixth week in Lent (fols. 73ra-85vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 11.) II-8) Homily by Jacob (ʾabbā Yāʿqob) for the Sunday of the sixth week in Lent (fols. 85vb-91ra) (Orient. 8192, 12.) II-9) Homily by the Orthodox for Palm Sunday (fols. 91ra-97ra) (cp. Orient. 8192, 13.) II-10) Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for reading at the service on Holy Thursday (fols. 97ra-103vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 14.) II-11) Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for Easter Eve (fols. 103vb-107vb) (cp. Orient. 8192, 15., beginning in the same way), in full II-12) Homily by the Orthodox for Easter Sunday (fols. 107vb-118va) (cp. Orient. 8192, 16.), in full II-13) Homily on the Suffering of Jesus Christ for Monday in Easter-week (fols. 118vb-122va, fol. 122 is loose), incomplete (cp. Orient. 8192, 17.)

msItem.title: Homilies
Homily by Jacob for the third Sunday in Lent
Homily on Lent, for the third Sunday in Lent
Homily by John Chrysostom for the fourth Sunday in Lent
Homily by the Orthodox on Lent, for the fourth Sunday in Lent
Homily by Gregory of Antioch for the fifth Sunday in Lent
Homily for the Friday of the sixth week in Lent
Homily for the Saturday of the sixth week in Lent
Homily by Jacob (ʾabbā Yāʿqob) for the Sunday of the sixth week in Lent
Homily by the Orthodox for Palm Sunday
Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for reading at the service on Holy Thursday
Homily by ʾabbā ʾEfrem for Easter Eve
Homily by the Orthodox for Easter Sunday
Homily on the Suffering of Jesus Christ for Monday in Easter-week

handNote.seg: Hand b, same as hand e of UM-045

msContents.summary: III) Martyrdom of St. George of Lydda (fols. 98rv, 142r-148v)

msItem.title: Martyrdom of St. George of Lydda
msItem.note: (cp. E.A.W. BUDGE, The History of George of Lydda, the patron Saint of England, London 1930, respectively pp. 89 l.17-91 l. 4 and pp. 96 l. 12-108 l. 18)

handNote.seg: Hand c, same as hand d of UM-045

msContents.summary: IV-1) Homily by Cyril (Gerlos) Patriarch of Jerusalem on the Resurrection of Jesus Christ (fol. 124r-131v, after the incipit page, the biggest part possibly missing), hand d (25 lines) IV-2) Discourse by John Chrysostom on the life and decollation of John the Baptist (fols. 126r-132v), possibly almost complete, hand d IV-3) Maṣḥafa lǝdatā la-Māryām “Book of the Nativity of Mary” (fols. 135, 136va-141v), end missing, hand d IV-4) Martyrdom of Theocritus the Reader, commemorated on 17 Maggābit (fol. 136r-va), end part and explicit, hand d

msItem.title: Homily by Cyril (Gerlos) Patriarch of Jerusalem on the Resurrection of Jesus Christ
Discourse by John Chrysostom on the life and decollation of John the Baptist
Martyrdom of Theocritus the Reader, commemorated on 17 Maggābit
Maṣḥafa lǝdatā la-Māryām “Book of the Nativity of Mary”
msItem.note: end missing. (Cp. M. CHAINE, Apocrypha de Beata Maria Virgine, Romae 1909 [CSCO 39, 40 (SAe 22, 23)], “Liber nativitatis Mariae”; the correct sequence is fols. 136va-141v, 135)

msContents.summary: V-1) A homily for Easter (fol. 133rv), ancient hand e V-2) Unidentified homily (fol. 152rv), ancient hand e (31 lines) V-3) Homily by John Chrysostom on Easter Sunday (fols. 156ra-161ra), in full (?), ancient hand e (32 lines) (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 17, HamSixBerl 1, 298-99) V-4) Homily by an anonymous author on Easter (fols. 161ra-161vb), 16th-cent. hand k; the text is written in empty space after Text V-3, and may be the beginning of the following text (which had been acephalous, originally or though the loss of leaves) V-5) Unidentified homily (fols. 162r-167v), acephalos (but cp. above), ancient hand e (31 lines) V-6) Unidentified homily (fols. 171r-171v), end part and explicit, ancient hand e (31 lines) V-7) A homily of Jacob (of Sarug?) (fol. 177vb), heading only, ancient hand e V-8) Unidentified homily (fols. 172ra-173ra), explicit, ancient hand e V-8) Homily by John Chrysostom on penance (fols. 173ra-185v), ancient hand e (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 25, HamSixBerl 1, 300) V-9) Unidentified homily (fols. 188ra-189rb), end part and explicit, ancient hand e (31 lines) V-10) Homily by Jacob of Serug on the Apostle Thomas (fols. 189r-194v), in full (?), ancient hand e (cp. EAE III, 262b) V-11) Homily by John Chrysostom on Lazarus (fols. 194va-197rb), in full (?), ancient hand e (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 20, HamSixBerl 1, 299; cp. EAE III, 293b) V-12) Homily by John Chrysostom on the Samaritan woman (cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 21, HamSixBerl 1, 299; Orient. 774 fols. 126r-131r, EAE III, 293b), in full (?), ancient hand e V-13) Homily by John Theologus (Tewologos) on Easter Sunday (fols. 205vb-211vb), end and some fols. missing, ancient hand e (cp. Orient. 774, no. 22).

msItem.title: Homiliary
A homily for Easter
Unidentified homily
Homily by John Chrysostom on Easter Sunday
Unidentified homily
msItem.note: acephalous, but the beginning may have been supplied in Additio 1

msItem.title: Unidentified homily
A homily of Jacob (of Sarug?)
Unidentified homily
Homily by John Chrysostom on penance
msItem.note: complete; cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 25, HamSixBerl 1, 300

msItem.title: Unidentified homily
Homily by Jacob of Serug on the Apostle Thomas
Homily by John Chrysostom on Lazarus
msItem.note: cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 20, HamSixBerl 1, 299; cp. EAE III, 293b

msItem.title: Homily by John Chrysostom on the Samaritan woman
msItem.note: cp. BerPrKult orient. fol. 3075 no. 21, HamSixBerl 1, 299; Orient. 774 fols. 126r-131r, EAE III, 293b

msItem.title: Homily by John Theologus (Tewologos) on Easter Sunday
msItem.note: end and some fols. missing; cp. Orient. 774, no. 22

item.desc: Homily by an anonymous author on Easter, added in 16th-cent. hand k; the text is written in empty space after Text V-3, and may be the beginning of the following text (which had been acephalous, originally or though the loss of leaves)

msItem.title: Books of Kings
2 Kings 6:7-13

handNote.seg: Hand f, ancient (similar but not identical to hand a of UM-045)

msContents.summary: Text concerning ʾabbā Yoḥannǝs (?) (fol. 149rv): hand g (28 lines)
VIII-1) The Finding of the Holy Cross by St Helena (?) (fols. 150r-152r, explicit), hand h (35 lines)
IX-1) 2 Kings 18:32-19:15 (fol. 153rv), ancient hand j (35 lines) (identical to the hand of UM-058, the text follows on UM-058 fol. 66rv)

msItem.title: Books of Kings
msItem.note: This part belongs to MS Bǝḥerāwi Kǝllǝlāwi Mangǝśti Tǝgrāy, ʿUrā Qirqos, UM-058; the text follows on Bǝḥerāwi Kǝllǝlāwi Mangǝśti Tǝgrāy, ʿUrā Qirqos, UM-058, 66 fol. 66rv.

handNote.seg: Hand j, ancient (identical to the hand of Bǝḥerāwi Kǝllǝlāwi Mangǝśti Tǝgrāy, ʿUrā Qirqos, UM-058, handNote h1)

msContents.summary: X) Text concerning Archangel Michael, the Vision of Daniel and the Three Holy Children (fol. 168r-169v), hand l (28 lines)

msItem.title: Text concerning Archangel Michael, the Vision of Daniel and the Three Holy Children

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: transformed from mycore to TEI P5
split msparts (only few IDs provided to works)
